show.title: The Big Lebowski
show.headline: The Coen Bros.' slacker odyssey really ties the room together

event.title: <show-title>The Big Lebowski</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: It's the greatest movie party ever, but that's just like, uh, our opinion, man
event.description:
You want a movie party? We can get you a movie party, believe me. There are ways, Dude. You don't wanna know about it, believe me.

With its endlessly quotable dialogue, twist-filled mystery, and iconic characters, THE BIG LEBOWSKI has become a fixture of modern pop culture. That’s why we’re putting together a movie party to celebrate the greatness that is El Duderino (we’re not into the whole “brevity” thing).

When you arrive, we’ll provide you with a "F**k It, Let's Go Bowling" bumper sticker to commemorate the forever-two-steps-behind Donny Kerabatsos, a notepad like Jackie Treehorn's, and rolling papers.

So throw on your favorite shabby sweater and roll on down for THE BIG LEBOWSKI Movie Party. It’ll be far out.

show.title: The Mummy Returns: 25th Anniversary
show.headline: Do you smell what the Scorpion King is cooking?

event.title: <show-title>The Mummy Returns: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Do you smell what the Scorpion King is cooking?
event.description:
Director Stephen Sommers’ THE MUMMY RETURNS is not only the action-packed follow-up to his beloved 1999 original, but it also marks the big screen debut of one Dwayne Douglas “The Rock” Johnson. Nine years after condemning Imhotep (Arnold Vosloo) back to the underworld, Rick (Brendan Fraser) and Evelyn (Rachel Weisz) have settled down, had a quick-witted kid, and are living their best adventurer lives. Unfortunately for them, a reincarnated version of Anck-su-namun (Patricia Velasquez) and a few more Imhotep-worshiping knuckleheads are hellbent on bringing the mummy back so that he can claim the unstoppable power of an ancient warlord, the Scorpion King (Johnson). Cue the desert-trotting adventure.

While not as revered as its predecessor, THE MUMMY RETURNS is still a grand journey with big thrills and some laugh-out-loud moments. Fraser and Weisz bring charm in a big way, and it’s fun to see The Rock in a movie before he became one of the biggest stars in Hollywood – even if he’s covered in CGI that isn’t, like, the greatest ever. That’s all part of the charm though. Plus, Anubis’ army of jackal-headed warriors is still rad as hell.

Your ticket includes the following props:

  • Black snake – The perfect weapon to throw at your enemies.
  • Scorpion bracelet – Don't worry, you can wear this for more than seven days with no issues.
  • Medjai sticker – This is a symbol of your sacred duty.
  • Combat mask – Wearing this may help you unlock memories of your past life.

show.title: Paying For It

show.title: Dolly
show.headline: Mommy knows best

event.title: <show-title>Dolly</show-title> <event-type-title>in 35mm</event-type-title>
event.headline: The latest in backwoods horror.
event.description:
Chase and Macy drop off Chase’s daughter, Evy, and then hike to a nearby overlook where he plans to propose. Those plans are soon forgotten when they discover a weird ‘art installation’ of dirty, broken dolls.

Unfortunately the dolls are actually more of an altar to the victims of Dolly—a violent, hulking, child-like person in a tattered dress and a porcelain doll mask—who arrives to bury the decapitated corpse of their latest plaything. While they have little interest in Chase outside of violence, Macy catches their eye and is taken home to become their newest doll.

With the help of a voice in the walls, Macy quickly realizes she must navigate her captor’s delusions and family trauma to have any hope of surviving the nightmare and the filthy house she finds herself in. Of course that means playing her role of a doll—including diaper changes and feedings—until she can find the right moment to make her move.

Nothing is ever quite as it seems, except of course for the absolutely goopy practical effects in this lo-fi throwback to the isolated murder families of THE HILLS HAVE EYES and the original TEXAS CHAIN SAW MASSACRE. Director Rod Blackhurst combines his clear inspiration with a uniquely demented point of view, building on his previous work, including HERE ALONE and writing NIGHT SWIM. Fabianne Therese gives a strong final girl performance as Macy, squaring off beautifully to transmasc wrestler Max the Impaler’s unspeaking Dolly, both of whom are joined by pitch-perfect performances from Seann William Scott and Ethan Suplee. (KEV DOOLEY)

35mm print courtesy of Independent Film Company and Shudder

show.title: The Driller Killer
show.headline: Abel Ferrara's Dirty Old New York shocker.

event.title: <show-title>The Driller Killer</show-title>
event.headline: Abel Ferrara's Dirty Old New York shocker.
event.description:
Showing an even seedier side of 1970s New York than TAXI DRIVER, Abel Ferrara’s landmark art-horror debut provides a look back to when Manhattan was an unforgiving labyrinth, a city of sleaze-inciting panic. Starving artist Reno (played by director Ferrara) lives in a squalid tenement and is plagued with nightmarish visions. Tenuously clinging to sanity, Reno loses it when a punk band moves in next door and he takes to the streets to vent his rage . . . with a power drill. DRILLER KILLER strikes an impressive balance between punk atmosphere and horror shocks. And as a bonus, the movie features some of the most repulsive on-screen pizza eating that you'll ever see.

DCP courtesy of Arrow Films and the American Genre Film Archive

show.title: The Coca-Cola Kid
show.headline: A subversive comic masterpiece from the director of SWEET MOVIE.

event.title: <show-title>The Coca-Cola Kid</show-title>
event.headline: A subversive comic masterpiece from the director of SWEET MOVIE.
event.description: When a sharp marketing exec from Coca Cola takes aim at a small town soda maker in Australia, he finds bullets headed in his direction. Eric Roberts leads with confident charm as Becker, the American golden boy envoyed Down Under to ensure that Coke’s offices there are sufficiently covering the map with its sugary soft drink. His strutting unintentionally draws the attention of Terri (Greta Scacchi), a sultry secretary with bright clunky earrings and ex-husband drama. Directed by Dušan Makavejev (WR: MYSTERIES OF THE ORGANISM), this is a movie of delightfully odd humor and feathery pillow fight surprises.

show.title: High Plains Drifter
show.headline: They'd never forget the day he drifted into town

event.title: <show-title>High Plains Drifter</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Bob Odenkirk and Ben Wheatley, ahead of their new film NORMAL
event.description:
HIGH PLAINS DRIFTER is presented as part of our Bob Odenkirk and Ben Wheatley Guest Selects for their new film, NORMAL.

"Who are you?" the dwarf Mordecai (Billy Curtis) asks Clint Eastwood's Stranger at the end of Eastwood's 1973 western HIGH PLAINS DRIFTER.

"You know," he replies, before vanishing into the desert heat waves near California's Mono Lake.

Adapting the amorally enigmatic and violent Man With No Name persona from his films with Sergio Leone, Eastwood's second film as director begins as his drifter emerges from that heat haze and rides into the odd lakefront settlement of Lago. Lago's residents are not particularly friendly, but once the Stranger shows his skills as a gunfighter, they beg him to defend them against a group of outlaws (led by Eastwood regular Geoffrey Lewis) who have a score to settle with the town. He agrees to train them in self-defense, but Mordecai and the innkeeper's wife, Sarah Belding (Verna Bloom), soon suspect that the Stranger has another, more personal agenda. By the time the Stranger makes the corrupt community paint their town red and rename it "Hell," it is clear that he is not just another gunslinger.

With its fragmented flashbacks and bizarre, austere locations, HIGH PLAINS DRIFTER's stylistic eccentricity lends an air of unsettling eeriness to its revenge story, adding an uncanny slant to Eastwood's antiheroic westerner. Seminal western hero John Wayne was so offended by Eastwood's harshly revisionist view of a frontier town that he wrote to Eastwood, objecting that this was not what the spirit of the West was all about. Eastwood's audience, however, was not so put off, and an exhibitors' poll named Eastwood a top box-office draw for 1973.

show.title: The Getaway (1972)
show.headline: A recently-released ex-con and his wife go on the run after a heist goes awry

event.title: <show-title>The Getaway (1972)</show-title>
event.headline: Programmed by Bob Odenkirk and Ben Wheatley, ahead of their new film NORMAL
event.description:
THE GETAWAY (1972) is presented as part of our Bob Odenkirk and Ben Wheatley Guest Selects for their new film, NORMAL.

A bank-robbing, car-chasing, back-stabbing crime thriller soaked in Texas sweat and blood! Steve McQueen and Ali MacGraw play a husband and wife heist team stuck in the middle of a rather complicated plan to knock over a bank that doesn’t go exactly as they meticulously planned. Beautifully directed by masterful (and reportedly very drunk) director Sam Peckinpah with a script by the great Walter Hill, THE GETAWAY started a real-life romance between its co-stars McQueen and MacGraw that ended up in marriage. Featuring rip-roaring and scenic car chases in the local surroundings of San Marcos, San Antonio, and Alpine, THE GETAWAY is a true Texas classic!

show.title: Céline and Julie Go Boating
show.headline: Alice in Wonderland meets French New Wave

event.title: <show-title>Céline and Julie Go Boating</show-title>
event.headline: Jacques Rivette’s magical mystery tour of female friendship and whimsy
event.description:
“I always let myself be overcome by irrepressible waves of feeling.”

Does it ever feel like you and your closest friend live in your own little universe? Inside jokes no one else would understand, secret languages born of meaningless moments, the strange ability to communicate telepathically with a simple glance. Some relationships, romantic or platonic, have a mystical air, a touch of destiny. In Jacques Rivette's CÉLENE AND JULIE GO BOATING, magic brings two young women, Célene (Juliet Berto), a cabaret magician, and Julie (Dominique Labourier), a librarian, together and leads them down the rabbit hole, if you will, into a mysterious world of absurdity and *GASP* murder. To quote critic Beatrice Loayza, the pair engage in “play as a life force.” Célene and Julie take turns swapping identities, move in together (U-Hauling lol), and traverse time and space through a “house of fiction” with the help of magical candy…. There’s a lot going on here, and it’s all so very French!

A celebration of female friendship and “pleasure as a form of liberation,” CÉLENE AND JULIE GO BOATING is a remarkable film filled to the brim with imagination and disruption. Think BEING JOHN MALKOVICH with less existential dread, or MULHOLLAND DRIVE with less existential dread. Inspired by Alice in Wonderland, Věra Chytilová’s DAISIES, and the works of Marcel Proust and Jean Cocteau, CÉLENE AND JULIE GO BOATING brings whimsy and joyful femininity to the French New Wave, and it is a must-see for anyone who has ever wanted to follow an impulse beyond the threshold of expectation. It doesn’t hurt to fall off the moon.

show.title: Melancholia
show.headline: A mysterious new planet threatens to collide with Earth

event.title: <show-title>Melancholia</show-title>
event.headline: A mysterious new planet threatens to collide with Earth
event.description:
On what should be the happiest day of her life, Justine (Kirsten Dunst) is grappling with severe depression. Her sister, Claire (Charlotte Gainsbourg), is trying her best to help her sister through the wedding, but her help comes across as controlling because her husband is paying for the whole extravaganza and has a rigid schedule. However, everything begins to unravel as a mysterious blue planet called Melancholia hurls towards the Earth.

Director Lars Von Trier developed MELANCHOLIA during a therapy session, when his therapist noted that depressed people tend to act calm during high-pressure events because they already anticipate the worst. Possibly his most accessible yet personal film in his oeuvre, MELANCHOLIA is a deeply beautiful examination of the human psyche under pressure.

show.title: Romancing in Thin Air
show.headline: Rarified love in Shangri-La

event.title: <show-title>Romancing in Thin Air</show-title>
event.headline: Rarified love in Shangri-La
event.description:
After being dumped publicly at the altar by his co-star, famous actor Michael (Louis Koo) falls into a drunken stupor that leads him to hide away in a truck that whisks him up into the snowy mountains of Shangri-La. The truck in question belongs to Sue (Sammi Cheng Sau-Man), a widow in denial that her late husband, who got lost in the woods nearby, is gone forever. As Michael starts healing himself in the tranquility of Sue’s quaint motel, he begins to fall in love with her.

ROMANCING IN THIN AIR director Johnnie To is most well known for his action and crime thrillers; his foray into melodramatic romance – a subgenre that is a staple in East Asian cinema – is touching, sweet, and deeply melancholic. Although To’s film definitely hits all the tropes that define romantic dramas, it’s also a love letter to storytelling and how cinema draws people to each other.

show.title: Hush (2016)
show.headline: Silence can be killer

event.title: <show-title>Hush (2016)</show-title> <event-type-title>The Shush Cut</event-type-title>
event.headline: Witness the black-and-white "Shush" cut of Mike Flanagan's cult classic
event.description:
Screening the black-and-white "Shush" cut

In this minimalist ode to John Carpenter’s HALLOWEEN, horror powerhouse Mike Flanagan tells the tale of a deaf author (Kate Siegel) who lives virtually isolated from the outside world. After losing her hearing in her teens, she’s created a silent safe place to live her life. That is, until one day she sees a masked face outside of her window who threatens to breach this zone. What ensues is a game of cat-and-mouse where the line between hunter and prey is blurred after Maddie decides to take a stand.

show.title: The Sacrament
show.headline: Director Ti West's found-footage remote commune-set horror-thriller

event.title: <show-title>The Sacrament</show-title>
event.headline: Director Ti West's found-footage remote commune-set horror-thriller
event.description: Two journalists set out to document their friend's journey to reunite with his estranged sister. They track her to an undisclosed location where they are welcomed into the remote world of “Eden Parish,” a self-sustained rural utopia composed of nearly two hundred members and overseen by a mysterious leader known only as “Father.” It quickly becomes evident to the newcomers that this paradise may not be as it seems. Eden Parish harbors a twisted secret. What started as just another documentary shoot soon becomes a fight for survival.

show.title: Crumb
show.headline: A portrait of an underground artist.

event.title: <show-title>Crumb</show-title>
event.headline: Terry Zwigoff's portrait of an underground artist.
event.description:
David Lynch presents one of the most critically acclaimed films ever made. A hilarious and mysterious journey through artistic genius and sexual obsession, CRUMB is a wild ride through the mind of Robert Crumb; creator of Zap Comix, Mr. Natural and Fritz the Cat. CRUMB enters a territory as spooky as it is fascinating... a portrait of the artist as misanthrope, as bad-boy visionary, as a joker and sex maniac and, finally, as hero. One of those rare film experiences that has the giddy effect of being a nightmare and a party at the same time. (Sony Pictures Classics)

4k DCP courtesy of Sony Pictures Classics

show.title: Mind Game
show.headline: Masaaki Yuasa's hilarious, trippy journey to heaven and back

event.title: <show-title>Mind Game</show-title>
event.headline: Masaaki Yuasa's hilarious, trippy journey to heaven and back.
event.description:
Presented in Japanese with English subtitles

Buckle in and prepare to surrender yourself to an exhilarating and wildly entertaining ride. Cult classic MIND GAME is an explosion of unconstrained expression with gloriously colorful images ricocheting in rapid-fire associations, like Masaaki Yuasa’s brain splattered onto the screen in all its goopy glory.

Audiences will begin to grasp what they are in for early on as loser Nishi, too wimpy to try to save his childhood sweetheart from gangsters, is shot in the butt by a soccer-playing psychopath, projecting Nishi into the afterlife. In this limbo, God, shown as a series of rapidly changing characters, tells him to walk toward the light. But Nishi runs like hell in the other direction and returns to Earth a changed man, driven to live each moment to the fullest. (GKIDS)

DCP courtesy of GKIDS

show.title: Riverbend
show.headline: Your new favorite exploitation rediscovery, newly restored.

event.title: <show-title>Riverbend</show-title>
event.headline: Your new favorite exploitation rediscovery, newly restored.
event.description:
A long‑lost classic of Black independent cinema returns. Led by Steve James in his first starring role and Margaret Avery in a compelling performance following her Oscar®‑nominated turn in THE COLOR PURPLE, this Texas-shot film follows three Vietnam veterans who spark an uprising against a racist Southern sheriff. Blending Blaxploitation grit with the moral force of SEVEN SAMURAI, this newly rediscovered work — restored to its brilliance from the original 35mm interpositive under the supervision of director Sam Firstenberg — is ready for a new generation.

DCP courtesy of Reelblack Renaissance

show.title: Mermaid (2026)
show.headline: Florida man saves injured mermaid and nurses her back to health

event.title: <show-title>Mermaid (2026)</show-title> <event-type-title>Sneak Preview</event-type-title>
event.headline: Be among the first to see this comedy about a Florida man who saves a mermaid
event.description: Doug is an awkward Florida drug addict. Divorced and alone, he shares custody of his young daughter with his ex. Doug's reeling from losing his job at a local strip club, where he takes care of the fish in the aquarium. Things are beyond bleak, so Doug zips his boat out into the Gulf to end the pain of his reality. But at his lowest point, he discovers an injured mermaid creature clinging to life. For Doug, it's a sign from the Universe, so he brings her home, gives her shelter in his tub, and begins to nurse her back to health. He even names her Destiny. As their rapport blossoms, word of his secret gets out and he is forced to protect his new friend by any means necessary.

Part 16 | 60 mins.
Cooper regains his identity; a showdown looms. Diane's true nature is revealed, and Dougie says goodbye to his family.

Part 17 | 60 mins.
Cooper confronts the ultimate evil; the truth about Laura Palmer is revealed. The final showdown with Mr. C occurs, and Judy's role is hinted at.

Part 18 | 60 mins.
Cooper's journey comes to a surreal and ambiguous conclusion. Cooper and Diane cross into another reality, leaving their fates uncertain.

show.title: F**k My Son! + Dance Freak
show.headline: A double feature of depravity

event.title: <show-title>F**k My Son! + Dance Freak</show-title> <event-type-title>Double Feature</event-type-title>
event.headline: A double feature of depravity
event.description:
DANCE FREAK contains several sequences with flashing lights that may affect those who are susceptible to photosensitive epilepsy or have other photosensitivities.

About FUCK MY SON!

Opening with a bargain-basement AMC theater pre-show complete with full-frontal dancing male nudity, Todd Rohal’s FUCK MY SON! adaptation is not for the faint of heart.

Expanding on Johnny Ryan’s underground comic of the same name, FUCK MY SON! is a tongue-in-cheek exploitation film in which everyone knows exactly what film they’re making.

It follows the plight of a young mother, out clothes shopping with her daughter Bernice, and teaching her hard life lessons as they catch a peeping tom watching them. If only that had been the hardest part of her day… Instead, the two try to help an old woman who’s fallen, only to quickly find the tables turned, as the old woman shoves the two in her van and kidnaps them to her secluded farmhouse.

Once there, with Bernice in a cage and the mother zip-tied to a pillar in a filthy basement, the old woman tries to make nice while she unveils her ultimate plan – to have the young woman fuck her son, at which point she’ll gladly let them go.

The nausea-inducing depravity that follows is balanced with a raunchy, bleak, absurdist humor, trademarks of both Johnny Ryan’s and Todd Rohal’s work.

Rohal is joined by long-time collaborators Steve Little and Robert Longstreet (as the old woman), along with an incredible performance from Tipper Newton as the young mother and a few other familiar faces along the way. The positively goopy practical makeup effects really bring this piece home and are provided by industry heavyweights Marcia King and Robert Kurtzman. All of this adds up to a not-to-be-missed spectacle. (Kev Dooley)

About DANCE FREAK

In a secret government facility, a team of scientists has done impossible, indescribable things. Genetic mutations, inter-dimensional experiments – nasty top-secret stuff. They played GOD. And like GOD, they are all probably dead. The filmmakers only had $45,000 to make this movie, and they ran out of that money almost immediately, so a lot of that stuff is just implied through dialogue. There is dancing, but it isn’t that crazy. Also, the movie gets loud sometimes, but it's not that long.

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.name: Baby Day Show
sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!

sessionAttributes.name: Kid Friendly
sessionAttributes.description: Grab the fam and let the fun begin! Our Kid Friendly screenings include pre-show activity sheets and crayons, a relaxed noise policy, and kids menus. Ages 3+ welcome with a parent or guardian.
